settings.title: Rebounding is a simple yet effective low-impact exercise that involves bouncing on a mini-trampoline.
Top Benefits Of Rebounding
settings.copy:
• Cardio: Stronger Heart
  • Weight Loss: Efficient Calorie Burn
  • Balance: Heightened Proprioception
  • Mental Health: Stress Relief
  • Wellness: Increased Lymphatic Flow
  • Recovery: Fast Muscle Recuperation
  • Fun Factor: Bouncing Brings Joy!

settings.title: Boosts Metabolism
settings.copy: Rebounding supercharges your metabolism, helping you burn calories even after your workout.

settings.title: Strengthens the Heart
settings.copy: Regular rebounding fortifies the heart and other muscles in the body, promoting overall cardiovascular health.

settings.title: Lowers Cholesterol
settings.copy: Rebounding can effectively lower circulating cholesterol and triglyceride levels, contributing to a healthier lipid profile.

settings.title: Improves Circulation
settings.copy: The exercise enhances blood circulation, which can contribute to better oxygen supply throughout the body.

settings.title: Prevents Chronic Edema/Swelling
settings.copy: Bouncing decreases the volume of blood pooling in the veins of the cardiovascular system.

settings.title: Manages Body Composition
settings.copy: Rebounding helps manage body composition and improves muscle-to-fat ratio, aiding in weight management

settings.title: Improves Endurance
settings.copy: Rebounding increases respiration and capacity for breathing, which can lead to improved lung health and endurance.

settings.title: Title
settings.copy: Copy

settings.title: Cardio & Weightloss
settings.copy: Rebounding is a low impact, total body workout that enhances your cardiovascular health and aids in weight loss. It boosts metabolism, burns calories, and strengthens the heart muscle.
settings.chart_title: Key cardiovascular and weight loss benefits:

settings.title: Improves Coordination
settings.copy: Rebounding improves coordination between the proprioceptors in the joints, enhancing balance and motor skills.

settings.title: Boosts Mental Performance
settings.copy: Rebounding can lead to better mental performance, sharpening your learning processes and improving memory.

settings.title: Reduces Anxiety
settings.copy: Rebounding can reduce feelings of anxiety and depression by increasing endorphins, the body’s natural mood boosters.

settings.title: Stimulates the Vestibular System
settings.copy: Rebounding stimulates the vestibular system, which controls balance, contributing to improved stability and coordination.

settings.title: Enhances Self-Image
settings.copy: Rebounding is an effective form of exercise that gives you a greater sense of control and an improved self-image—while having fun!

settings.title: Improves Cognitive Function
settings.copy: Rebounding can help increase blood flow to the brain, delivering oxygen and nutrients that support optimal cognitive performance.

settings.title: Balance & Mental Health
settings.copy: Rebounding can have several positive effects on mental health, thanks to its unique combination of physical activity, stress reduction, and enjoyment. Furthermore, it enhances proprioception and equilibrium, which are essential for maintaining and improving balance and coordination.
settings.chart_title: Key balance and mental well-being benefits::

settings.title: Increases Lymphatic Drainage
settings.copy: Rebounding stimulates the millions of one-way valves in the lymphatic system, naturally helping flush out excess fluids and toxins.

settings.title: Promotes Tissue Repair
settings.copy: Rebounding promotes tissue repair and protects the joints from the chronic fatigue and impact delivered by exercising on hard surfaces.

settings.title: Improves Sleep
settings.copy: Rebounding allows for deeper sleep and easier relaxation, contributing to overall wellness.

settings.title: Keeps You Young
settings.copy: Rebounding slows down atrophy in the aging process, helping you stay young and vibrant.

settings.title: Relieves Pain
settings.copy: Rebounding can relieve pain from sore neck and back, headaches, and discomfort caused by lack of exercise. It’s easier on joints than the impact of running.

settings.title: Increases Energy Levels
settings.copy: Rebounding combats feelings of fatigue and lethargy, through the combination of cardiovascular activity and oxygen circulation, so you feel more energized and alert.

settings.title: Naturally Boosts Immune System
settings.copy: Rebounding can help reduce the number of colds, allergies, digestive disturbances, and abdominal problems

settings.title: Wellness & Recovery
settings.copy: Rebounding plays a significant role in overall wellness, most notably how it improves lymphatic flow. Additionally, it allows the body to recover faster due to the low-impactive nature of the exercise.
settings.chart_title: Key balance and mental well-being benefits:

ACE-Sponsored Research
settings.copy: The 19-minute JumpSport exercise video featured motivational music and incorporated a variety of choreography and movements to create a full-body workout. Heart rates and VO2max were collected every minute and caloric expenditure was calculated from the VO2 data. RPE was also assessed every five minutes.”
settings.studies_title: More Studies About The Benefits of Rebounding
settings.disclaimer: Based on a PROSOURCE™: OCTOBER 2016 study of a 19-minute JumpSport exercise video with female college age participants

settings.title: How many minutes a day should I rebound?
settings.copy: Most fitness professionals recommend that you rebound at least 10 minutes per day. For a more effective workout, gradually increase your bounce time up to 60 minutes. Be sure to take breaks and stay hydrated.

settings.title: Does bouncing reduce belly fat?
settings.copy: Most fitness professionals recommend that you rebound at least 10 minutes per day. For a more effective workout, gradually increase your bounce time up to 60 minutes. Be sure to take breaks and stay hydrated.

settings.title: How long should I rebound for lymphatic drainage?
settings.copy: Most fitness professionals recommend that you rebound at least 10 minutes per day. For a more effective workout, gradually increase your bounce time up to 60 minutes. Be sure to take breaks and stay hydrated.
